title: [ExportVerilog] Unused Ports Generate Unused Wires
body:
If a submodule's output is unused we currently generate an output wire in each instantiation site for this to drive. However, I think it is reasonable to not generate this wire and instead, omit the port when hooking up the instance.

Consider:

```
circuit Foo:
  extmodule Bar:
    output a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>

  module Foo:
    output a: UInt<1>

    inst bar of Bar
    a <= bar.a
```

This currently compiles to:

```verilog
module Foo(
  output a);

  wire _bar_b;
  Bar bar (
    .a (a),
    .b (_bar_b)
  );
endmodule
```

However, it seems fine to remove `_bar_b` and instead emit:
```verilog
module Foo(
  output a);

  Bar bar (
    .a (a),
    .b (/* unused */)
  );
endmodule
```
